title: QA Unequal-k CCR Invariant: T_F propagator on S_9 with one witness per orbit class (Cosmos, Satellite, Singularity); equal-k delta limit, orbit decomposition, periodicity, and Lehmann trace verified exhaustively
source_attribution: Mannheim, P.D., 'Equivalence of light-front quantization and instant-time quantization,' Phys. Rev. D 102 025020 (2020), arXiv:1909.03548 — unequal-time commutator as c-number invariant (eq 2.2) and Lehmann spectral representation (eqs 8.9, 8.16). Companion cert [260] QA Orbit-Dirac Bracket delivers MC-3/MC-4 (Blaschke-Gieres eqs 5.32-5.39). Cross-map docs/theory/QA_QFT_ETCR_CROSSMAP.md §4.1 (MC-1, MC-2). Prior-art audit docs/theory/QA_QFT_COMMUTATORS_PRIOR_ART.md §5a (identifies C10 equal-k CCR as slice-dependent, missing the unequal-k invariant). Paper section papers/in-progress/qft-etcr-orbit-quotient/section.md §3 and §6. Precision guardrail: the equal-k delta-function CCR [a_{b,e}, a_dag_{b',e'}] = delta_{b,b'} delta_{e,e'} is a proposed observer-side canonical encoding, NOT derived from QA orbit dynamics. The cert certifies only the unequal-k invariant and its internal consistency. Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-21.

dynamics.note: Fibonacci-like QA step. Path time k is the non-negative integer step index.

propagator_definition.name: i_Delta_QA
propagator_definition.role: QA-layer analog of Mannheim's unequal-time c-number commutator i_Delta(x-y) eq (2.2). Depends on |Delta_k| only — slice-independent. Integer-valued — Theorem NT compliant.

stipulated_observer_side_encoding.status: proposed observer-side canonical encoding; NOT derived from QA

lehmann_trace_formula.note: Sum over orbit classes of |O| * 1[period(O) divides Delta_k]. Each orbit class contributes its density iff Delta_k is a multiple of its period. The Singularity contributes 1 for every Delta_k (period 1 divides everything).

witnesses.note: Fibonacci Cosmos orbit on S_9 at I=1. Trajectory from (1,1) traverses 24 distinct pairs under T_F and returns to (1,1).
Ninbonacci Singularity fixed point on S_9: T_F(9, 9) = (a1(18), 9) = (9, 9).

scope_boundaries.covered: m=9, T_F dynamics, exhaustive equal-k limit check over {1..9}^2 x {1..9}^2, orbit decomposition across all 5 orbits of S_9, periodicity verified at the full-propagator level for each witness, Lehmann trace verified at Delta_k in {0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24}
not_covered: m=24 — deferred to v2; period structure on S_24 is {1, 3, 6, 8, 12, 24} across 30 orbits, Lehmann formula generalizes but the trace decomposition has more terms

Alternative step operators beyond T_F — Lucas T_L, Tribonacci step, etc. Each defines its own orbit structure and propagator; slice-independence across T-operator choice is a deeper invariant not part of v1

Interacting case / Lehmann spectral density rho(sigma^2) integral form (Mannheim eq 8.9/8.16) — QA analog would introduce a 'mass spectrum' via orbit-period distribution; v1 uses the free-case trace decomposition only
Derivation of the stipulated equal-k CCR from QA primitives — out of scope by design; cert certifies unequal-k invariant only
